title: QR Code Item tracking App
excerpt:
Hi there :wave:, 
Skiply is a Monday.com customer for years, and we decided to start building Apps with the Monday App Challenge. 
The QR Code ITem Tracking App answers to a need that we had internally in the factory. 
Y…

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there ,

Skiply is a Monday.com customer for years, and we decided to start building Apps with the Monday App Challenge.

The QR Code ITem Tracking App answers to a need that we had internally in the factory.

You can follow an order or an asset just by scanning 2 codes. For instance, when the order arrives in the shipping zone, the worker scan the order QR Code, and then, he scan the QR code associate with the shipping zone. It can update a status column, or a text column, depending on the recipe.

Your worker spare time, because they do not need to have a working station or to connect directly to Monday.com in the workshop. And you spare money, because equivalent systems cost a lot (specific scanner linked to ERPs).

You need to register to a free Ubiqod account (see the doc).

We made a detailed documentation and presentation here: https://www.skiply.eu/en/monday-com-app-qr-code-item-tracking/

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml:
This looks very interesting.
What’s the pricing for this?

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @nealb,
This one is free if you use the status column only (= if you use the codes of the Monday board view). The free tier also includes 10 “text codes” from our backend (If you want to update a text column with a free text). Send me a PM if you need more.

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Heyooo @jeromeskiply

This is definitely an awesome app and I was very impressed seeing this released myself Awesome tool that makes monday.com even more versatile and flexible for our users - I love the TON of extra value you’ve added with this one.

-Alex

posts.author: snicho (Stephen Nicholson)
posts.cookedHtml:
This technology has so much application, not only in manufacturing, but also transport and logistics, as well as service providers across a huge range of industries.

Well done in bringing this technology to market!

I’m sure that many here can benefit from this particular integration that you’ve made available to the monday.com platform.

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there ,
We implemented a couple of new features after listening to user’s feedback:

  • You can now generate a direct QR Code Link to an item (that will open the item in the Mobile App for instance) like this: Generate a QR Code link to a Monday.com Item - YouTube
    This request was made by users who want to track orders in a factory or on construction sites. This feature doesn’t even request a Skiply account.
  • We add a new recipe that generates a tracking code when pressing a button (for status and test tracking)
  • We add the item a text at the bottom of the QR Code to avoid any confusion when printing it.

Cheers!

posts.author: gleurent (Leurent )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
@jeromeskiply
@AlexSavchuk
@snicho

Big thanks to Jerome from Skiply for their open mind, reactivity in order to find solution to our question initially ask to Monday team.
Using QR code printed on our construction drawing, we could find easy pulse on our mobile in order to update, upload document or photo, quality inspection report etc…It is very useful, specially when we are on construction site.
Many thanks!!!
Now we have to find a solution In order to print directly from monday sticker with QR code…

posts.author: Andrew.Oehlert (Andrew Oehlert)
posts.cookedHtml: We are exploring the possibility of adding this feature to our inventory management process. How many unique QR codes can be generated and used at one time? We have 2,500 items needing to be tracked at once.

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml: Sky is the limit. I sent you a private message.

posts.author: Yu1992 (Yumi)
posts.cookedHtml:
@jeromeskiply have you guys programmed a way to massively print out the QR codes that have been generated by each new item/row?

We would like to implement the use of QR’s, however, we would like to find a fast way to print (with a thermal labeler) out a large number of QRs without too much manipulation. I know you can download a zip file with all the QRs from a group, however opening each png inside the zip file seems very tedious.

Please let me know if you have any suggestions for this!

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Yu1992
@gleurent had the same need. We solved it by providing the url of the tracking link instead of the qr code itself (dedicated recipe). Then, they export it to Excel, and the printer generates the code from the link. Alternatively, you can use an Excel plugin to generate the qr code.
I hope this helps.

posts.author: briancowell (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @jeromeskiply !
Thanks for making this feature possible, it is amazingly helpful.

Curious if there is a way to update/upgrade the printable QR code to automatically include other fields from the pulse. It’s helpful that the pulse name is below the code, but if it were possible to include other fields, say ‘product description’ or other descriptive features of the product, that would take it over the top. Is this possible?

QR_image1068×730 32.4 KB

Thanks again,
brian

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @briancowell
Thank you for your feedback. I’m very happy that you find it helpful!
Adding the ability to choose which column to print under the code is technically possible… we just have to code it
We will add this one to the roadmap, but it may take time, as we received a lot of very good improvement suggestions like this one.
@Estelle for addition to the RM
Jerome

posts.author: briancowell (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you, @jeromeskiply
Appreciate the consideration to make a feature like this available! Looking forward to what the Skiply team continues to develop.
Cheers,
brian

posts.author: Yu1992 (Yumi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jeromeskiply ! How can we get the URL of the tracking link as oppose to the QR in png format?
We think we have found a good way to make automatized labels but in order to make it work, we will need the qr in url format.

image1344×668 69.9 KB

Is it option 2? From what I understand, itll generate both a QR in png format and a link, right? Will it have the same functionality as option 1 (when scanned, and then scan a qr generated by the board, it will update the status) ?

Thanks in advance!

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Yu1992 ,
Yes, option 2 is the solution! Same functionality as option 1.
Two differences:

  1. you have to click a button on the item to generate it (you must add a button column on the board).
  2. It cost one Ubiqod credit per operation. You still have 100 free credits per month.

posts.author: Yu1992 (Yumi)
posts.cookedHtml: @jeromeskiply aweesome! we’ll try it out!!

posts.author: shammel (Shane Hammel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Proof of Presence when status changed by use of QR code I have been in contact with Skiply to determine options of proof of presence related to change in status of board items. Our mobile workers use various boards daily to manage the workflows of routine tasks. I needed a way to determine if the workers used the QR code generated for each line items to change the status of an item (proof of presence) or if they just accessed the board and changed the item manually. Skiply’s team advised that if the code was used to change an item, that the person that created the codes for the board (me) would show within the activity log as the person that changed the item status. Alternatively, if the worker accessed the board normally (not using the QR code) they would display in the activity log as the person that changed the item status. This is a poor mans way to implement proof of presence using the Skiply QR code integration within Monday. Anyone else building on this idea please tag me or reply. A big thank you to the Skiply Team!

Cheers,
Shane

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks @shammel for your feedback! This is a great motivation source for the team.

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml: Version 1.1 is out! You can now generate the links automatically when an item is added. You can still use a button instead. Feedback welcome.

posts.author: meytalPeleg (Meytal Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! @jeromeskiply , great app! is there an option to create subitems by scanning QR? we will use it for food bank and wandering if the option will be available?

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @meytalPeleg,
Thank you!
Unfortunatly, for the moment you can’t use the App with subitems.
For technical reasons, I don’t think we will implement it soon. But I will let you know we change our plans.
Sorry for that

posts.author: Natalie79 (Nat Sun)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jeromeskiply, we have a lot of samples daily and we have to manually type them one by one onto our Monday.com. Can we create QR code for each SKU and monday.com will automatically save that data? The saved info must consist: (We can create a specific format in order to read the barcode into below?)

Style Name
Size
Sample Type
Date
Customer.

Thank you!! Very interested to know!

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Natalie79,
It could be a real game changer for monday.com in production! But unfortunatly, our App is not suitable for that. We do the follow-up of the items, but we do not manage the creation of items based on QR code content.

posts.author: YannD (Yann Dehuy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Natalie79

General caster is more suited to your needs

posts.author: scottym (Scott Mossburg)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @jeromeskiply we are experimenting with skiply qr code inventory. We have it set up where we can scan a qr code and directly update the monday.com board. Is there a way to set up the qr code where we can have a different department in our company scan the qr code and request inventory from our warehouse inventory?

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @scottym,
This is not possible at the moment as it is.
But if the person scanning have a monday.com account, you can use this feature: Generate a QR Code link to a Monday.com Item - YouTube (free of charge)

posts.author: dmitry_bzns (Dmitry)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @jeromeskiply, I was looking into implementing QR codes into our WMS system and I wanted to start with optimizing and speeding up order fulfillment process. I saw there’s a feature to scan QR code to update pulse status. I don’t want to generate tracking code, but just have a set of QR codes to update the status. Is there an option to do just that without utilizing Ubiqod credits? Thanks

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @dmitry_bzns!

I will be happy to help. Can you explain the process you would like to implement, I’m not sure to understand.

posts.author: dmitry_bzns (Dmitry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @jeromeskiply thanks for a reply.

We use Monday to track order fulfilment stages from picking → packing - > Shipping. Each status sends the update to Shopify’s customer account and there they can see what stage the order is in.

When fulfillment person scans the packing slip inside Monday’s search bar it brings the order. Then they have to use mouse to hover over the pulse tab and click to change the status.

I want them to scan the QR code instead of the mouse click to streamline the picking/packing flow.

So I was thinking if we could use something like a hotkey QR code that would be assigned to the certain pulse and by scanning it would update the status.

*** I just realized that when we scan packing slip it only searches the order, but not actually opening it as a separate window, so Monday wouldn’t know what item to update… In that case we would need to use item tracking QR code…Am I right?

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, you are right, you need our App

You can contact me in DM to discuss credit prices depending on the volume.

You have 100 free codes / month.

posts.author: Vestec (Stian Osmundsen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jeromeskiply
Do you have an update on this feature requested by @briancowell.
I would love to get it implemented in our workflow.

posts.author: jeromeskiply (Jerome Chambard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Vestec,
I don’t see it coming in the short term.
Sorry for that.

posts.author: bobbylurve (Rob)
posts.cookedHtml:
Very useful thread to read. Thanks all.

Has anyone found a solution for a good plug and play printer, that can just print the QR directly from Monday.com?

We don’t have a huge amount, maybe 20 at a time, so can easily click on the QR code on the board and press print.

The ideal would be to have that option direct from mobile or PC?? Hoping that someone else has already found a winning solution??

posts.author: DannyTank (DannyTank)
posts.cookedHtml:
I saw your post about the QR Code Item Tracking App and thought I’d share my thoughts. I think it’s amazing that you can streamline your process with just a few QR code scans. And kudos to you for finding a solution that saves time and money!
Have you considered using Smart Engines SDK for your QR code scanning needs? It’s a top-notch QR code scanning solution that could further enhance your app. Its fast and accurate scanning capabilities could make your process even smoother. Plus, its ability to integrate with various systems could be the perfect fit for your needs. Just something to consider!

posts.author: bobbylurve (Rob)

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

I’m happy to announce a release of QR Flow — vary flexible and affordable QR code tracking solution integrated with monday.

With QR Flow you can:

  1. Generate QR codes that trigger one or multiple automations upon scanning — including built-in and third-party automations!
  2. Collect user input after scanning (status, subitems, numbers) and automatically update the corresponding monday columns.
  3. Display selected item details after QR code is scanned.
  4. Print multiple QR codes via board view.
  5. Generate single-use QR codes that are automatically disabled after being scanned.
  6. … and more!

image1466×845 80.6 KB

image1473×694 61 KB

Still can’t find the right functionality? Contact us at contat@workflow-boost.com and we’ll assist you.
